summary: Dependency-confusion PoC exfiltrates host/user data to Burp Collaborator on install
details: Package declares a preinstall hook ("preinstall": "node index.js") that runs automatically on npm install. index.js collects installer-side identifiers — os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, os.homedir(), __dirname, dns.getServers(), and the contents of package.json — and POSTs them via https to a subdomain of oastify.com (Burp Collaborator, an out-of-band interaction service), specifically gi2dq59ku7d5xyjnjhuwpyr1gsmjaayz.oastify.com. The package version 99.99.99 and self-description as a 'Dependency Confusion POC' are consistent with a namespace-squat probe targeting an internal package name; the exfiltration fires on default install without any user action.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
